ogTitle: Tennyson vs Tennyson Point: Climate Safety

heading: Tennyson vs Tennyson Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tennyson, New South Wales and Tennyson Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tennyson and Tennyson Point have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Tennyson or Tennyson Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tennyson has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Tennyson Point scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tennyson scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Tennyson Point scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tennyson or Tennyson Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tennyson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
